biblical.framework: God's judgment on nations persisting in abominations or rejecting His covenant.
biblical.evidence: The long-delayed fulfillment of the curse on Canaan.

conventional.framework: A geopolitical necessity for Roman security ('Carthago delenda est').
conventional.evidence: Roman historical accounts of the Third Punic War.

comparisons.resolution: While Rome acted out of self-interest, biblically the destruction serves as the final judgment against Canaanite idolatry, showing God's sovereign orchestration of history.
